It also provides validation utilities to ensure values conform to expected ranges for latitude and longitude. Methods Denormalize(int, ValueType) Converts a normalized integer value to its denormalized double representation based on the specified type. public static double Denormalize(int value, PolylineEncoding.ValueType type) Parameters value int The normalized integer value to be denormalized. Must be within the valid range for the specified type. type PolylineEncoding.ValueType The type that defines the valid range for value. Returns double The denormalized double representation of the input value. Returns 0.0 if value is 0. Remarks The denormalization process divides the input value by a predefined precision factor to produce the resulting double. Ensure that value is validated against the specified type before calling this method. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if value is outside the valid range for the specified type. GetCharCount(int) Determines the number of characters required to represent the specified integer value within predefined variance ranges. public static int GetCharCount(int variance) Parameters variance int The integer value for which the character count is calculated. Must be within the range of a 32-bit signed integer. Returns int The number of characters required to represent the variance value, based on its magnitude. Returns a value between 1 and 6 inclusive. Remarks The method uses predefined ranges to efficiently determine the character count. Smaller values require fewer characters, while larger values require more. This method is optimized for performance using a switch expression. Normalize(double, ValueType) Normalizes a given numeric value based on the specified type and precision settings. public static int Normalize(double value, PolylineEncoding.ValueType type) Parameters value double The numeric value to normalize. Must be a finite number. type PolylineEncoding.ValueType The type against which the value is validated. Determines the acceptable range for the value. Returns int An integer representing the normalized value. Returns 0 if the input value is 0.0. Remarks This method validates the input value to ensure it is finite and within the acceptable range for the specified type. If the value is valid, it applies a normalization algorithm using a predefined precision factor. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if value is not a finite number or is outside the valid range for the specified type. TryReadValue(ref int, ref ReadOnlyMemory<char>, ref int) Attempts to read a value from the specified buffer and updates the variance. public static bool TryReadValue(ref int variance, ref ReadOnlyMemory<char> buffer, ref int position) Parameters variance int A reference to the integer that will be updated based on the value read from the buffer. buffer ReadOnlyMemory<char> A reference to the read-only memory buffer containing the data to be processed. position int A reference to the current position within the buffer. The position is incremented as the method reads data. Returns bool true if a value was successfully read and the end of the buffer was not reached; otherwise, false. Remarks This method processes the buffer starting at the specified position and attempts to decode a value. The decoded value is used to update the variance parameter. The method stops reading when a termination condition is met or the end of the buffer is reached. TryWriteValue(int, ref Span<char>, ref int) Attempts to write a value derived from the specified variance into the provided buffer at the given position. public static bool TryWriteValue(int variance, ref Span<char> buffer, ref int position) Parameters variance int The integer value used to calculate the output to be written into the buffer. buffer Span<char> A reference to the span of characters where the value will be written. position int A reference to the current position in the buffer where writing begins. This value is updated to reflect the new position after writing. Returns bool true if the value was successfully written to the buffer; otherwise, false. Remarks This method performs bounds checking to ensure that the buffer has sufficient space to accommodate the calculated value. If the buffer does not have enough space, the method returns false without modifying the buffer or position.
